phrase: I need to check my balance.
example: I need to check my balance before I make any big purchases today.

phrase: I'd like to make a deposit.
example: I'd like to make a deposit — I have a check and some cash.

phrase: I need to withdraw some cash.
example: I need to withdraw some cash — the place I'm going is cash only.

phrase: Is there a fee for that?
example: Is there a fee for that? I want to use an ATM on a different network.

phrase: I got charged an overdraft fee.
example: I got charged an overdraft fee — can you waive it just this once?

phrase: Can I set up direct deposit?
example: Can I set up direct deposit for my paycheck? What information do you need?

phrase: My card got declined.
example: My card got declined — that's strange, I know there's money in there.

phrase: I need to report a lost card.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə rɪˈpɔrt ə lɔst kɑrd/
example: I need to report a lost card — I think I left it at the restaurant last night.

phrase: Can you freeze my account?
example: Can you freeze my account? I think someone may have stolen my card number.

phrase: There's a suspicious charge on my account.
pronunciation: /ðɛrz ə səˈspɪʃəs tʃɑrdʒ ɑn maɪ əˈkaʊnt/
example: There's a suspicious charge on my account — I didn't buy anything from that store.

phrase: I'd like to dispute this charge.
example: I'd like to dispute this charge — I never received the item I paid for.

phrase: How long does a transfer take?
example: How long does a transfer take between two different banks?

phrase: I need to send money to someone.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə sɛnd ˈmʌni tə ˈsʌmˌwʌn/
example: I need to send money to someone — can I do that through Zelle?

phrase: I'll send it through Venmo.
example: I'll send it through Venmo — what's your username?

phrase: Split it evenly between us.
example: Split it evenly between us — thirty bucks each should cover it.

phrase: I'll get you back later.
example: I'll get you back later — I don't have cash on me right now.

phrase: You owe me twenty dollars.
example: You owe me twenty dollars from dinner last Tuesday — don't forget!

phrase: Is the tip included?
example: Is the tip included for large parties, or do we add it ourselves?

phrase: How much should I tip?
example: How much should I tip for a to-go order? I never know the etiquette.

phrase: Twenty percent is standard.
example: Twenty percent is standard for sit-down restaurants with good service.

phrase: I'm on a tight budget.
example: I'm on a tight budget this month — no unnecessary spending.

phrase: I need to cut back on spending.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə kʌt bæk ɑn ˈspɛndɪŋ/
example: I need to cut back on spending — I went way over my budget last month.

phrase: I'm saving up for a car.
example: I'm saving up for a car — trying to put aside five hundred a month.

phrase: I have automatic bill pay set up.
pronunciation: /aɪ hæv ˌɔtəˈmætɪk bɪl peɪ sɛt ʌp/
example: I have automatic bill pay set up so I never miss a due date.

phrase: My payment is due this Friday.
example: My payment is due this Friday — I need to make sure there's enough in the account.

phrase: I'm behind on my bills.
example: I'm behind on my bills — it's been a tough couple of months financially.

phrase: Can I set up a payment plan?
pronunciation: /kæn aɪ sɛt ʌp ə ˈpeɪmənt plæn/
example: Can I set up a payment plan? I can't pay the full amount all at once.

phrase: What's the interest rate?
example: What's the interest rate on this credit card? I want to avoid high APR.

phrase: I'm trying to build my credit score.
pronunciation: /aɪm ˈtraɪɪŋ tə bɪld maɪ ˈkrɛdɪt skɔr/
example: I'm trying to build my credit score — paying everything on time helps the most.

phrase: My credit score took a hit.
example: My credit score took a hit after I missed a payment last year.

phrase: I need to open a savings account.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə ˈoʊpən ə ˈseɪvɪŋz əˈkaʊnt/
example: I need to open a savings account — my checking is fine but I need to save more.

phrase: What documents do I need to open an account?
pronunciation: /wʌt ˈdɑkjəmənts du aɪ nid tə ˈoʊpən ən əˈkaʊnt/
example: What documents do I need to open an account? Just my ID and a deposit?

phrase: Is there a minimum balance?
example: Is there a minimum balance to avoid the monthly maintenance fee?

phrase: I need to cash a check.
example: I need to cash a check — do I need an account here to do that?

phrase: Can I mobile deposit this check?
example: Can I mobile deposit this check through the app, or do I have to come in?

phrase: How long until the funds clear?
example: How long until the funds clear? I need to use that money tomorrow.

phrase: I want to apply for a loan.
pronunciation: /aɪ wɑnt tə əˈplaɪ fər ə loʊn/
example: I want to apply for a loan — I'm trying to buy a used car.

phrase: What are the loan terms?
example: What are the loan terms? How many months to pay it off?

phrase: My monthly payment would be...
example: My monthly payment would be around two hundred — I think I can manage that.

phrase: I need to change my PIN.
example: I need to change my PIN — I think someone might have seen me enter it.

phrase: This ATM is out of service.
pronunciation: /ðɪs eɪ ti ɛm ɪz aʊt əv ˈsɜrvɪs/
example: This ATM is out of service — I'll try the one at the gas station down the road.

phrase: Where's the nearest ATM?
example: Where's the nearest ATM? I need to grab some cash before the market.

phrase: I prefer to pay cash.
example: I prefer to pay cash — it helps me stick to my budget better.

phrase: Do you have change for a twenty?
pronunciation: /du ju hæv tʃeɪndʒ fər ə ˈtwɛnti/
example: Do you have change for a twenty? I only have big bills on me right now.

phrase: The tax comes out to about eight percent.
pronunciation: /ðə tæks kʌmz aʊt tə əˈbaʊt eɪt pərˈsɛnt/
example: The tax comes out to about eight percent in Texas — no state income tax though.

phrase: I need to file my taxes.
example: I need to file my taxes before April fifteenth — I always wait until the last minute.

phrase: I'm getting a refund this year.
example: I'm getting a refund this year — I'm going to put it straight into savings.

phrase: I owe taxes this year.
example: I owe taxes this year — I didn't have enough withheld from my paychecks.

phrase: Can I speak to a banker?
example: Can I speak to a banker? I have some questions about my account options.

phrase: I need to update my address on file.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə ʌpˈdeɪt maɪ ˈædrɛs ɑn faɪl/
example: I need to update my address on file — I moved to San Marcos two months ago.

phrase: Money's been tight lately.
example: Money's been tight lately — I'm just trying to get through the month.

phrase: Can I get a cashier's check?
example: Can I get a cashier's check for the security deposit? The landlord requires it.

phrase: What's the wire transfer fee?
example: What's the wire transfer fee for sending money internationally?

phrase: I'd like to open a joint account.
pronunciation: /aɪd laɪk tə ˈoʊpən ə dʒɔɪnt əˈkaʊnt/
example: I'd like to open a joint account with my spouse — what do we both need to bring?

phrase: I'm trying to avoid overdraft fees.
example: I'm trying to avoid overdraft fees — can I link my savings as a backup?

phrase: How do I set up online bill pay?
pronunciation: /haʊ du aɪ sɛt ʌp ˈɑnˌlaɪn bɪl peɪ/
example: How do I set up online bill pay through the banking app?

phrase: I need to dispute an unauthorized charge.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə dɪˈspjut ən ʌnˈɔθəˌraɪzd tʃɑrdʒ/
example: I need to dispute an unauthorized charge — I've never heard of this merchant.

phrase: Can I see my transaction history?
example: Can I see my transaction history for the past thirty days?

phrase: I'd like to increase my credit limit.
pronunciation: /aɪd laɪk tə ɪnˈkris maɪ ˈkrɛdɪt ˈlɪmɪt/
example: I'd like to increase my credit limit — I've had the card for two years with no late payments.

phrase: My check bounced.
example: My check bounced — I didn't realize the funds hadn't cleared yet. I'm so embarrassed.

phrase: I'm saving for a down payment.
example: I'm saving for a down payment on a house — it takes a lot of discipline.

phrase: Can I link my accounts?
example: Can I link my accounts so I can transfer money between them easily?

phrase: What's my available credit?
example: What's my available credit on this card right now?

phrase: I'd like to close this account.
example: I'd like to close this account — I haven't used it in over a year.
